intro: Ivania cremnophila is a plant species, also known as Chinchay Huasca, that is native to the Andean region of Peru and belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ivania cremnophila include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
